noveltyJustification: This pass added the canonical-vs-provider model-id gap and the model-profile conflict with automatic fallback, while confirming the existing optional child 004 scope.
keyQuestions: Should fallback-router.ts be wired into real GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback now, or remain an offline/reference-only primitive for this phase?
answeredQuestions: Do not make fallback-router wiring a blocker for the core system-deep-loop merge or this read-only research phase. Keep it optional/operator-gated. If implemented, amend child 004 to add an explicit fallback registry/mapping surface, attach substitution policy in fanout-run.cjs or through a generic retry-exhausted hook, emit attribution events/labels, and test forced-failure GLM-to-MiMo re-dispatch.

ruledOut.approach: Treat fallback-router wiring as required for structural merge closeout
ruledOut.reason: Parent and child specs mark it optional/operator-gated, and current fanout mechanics already accept retry/salvage/manual re-dispatch for GLM failures.

ruledOut.approach: Hardcode GLM-to-MiMo substitution inside generic fanout-pool.cjs
ruledOut.reason: The generic pool knows retry state, while fanout-run owns model slugs, executor kind, lineage labels, and command construction.

graphEvents.label: Fallback router is ready but zero-caller
Fallback wiring remains optional for core merge

label: fallback-router.ts is mature pure routing code but has no fanout caller
Real GLM/MiMo fallback needs canonical-id to provider-slug mapping
Current model profiles document no automatic fallback
Fallback wiring should remain optional and non-blocking for the core merge

direction: Treat fallback-router wiring as mandatory merge closeout
reason: It changes fan-out behavior and is already scoped as optional/operator-gated child 004.
